response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack engineer, but his profile diverges from nearly every core requirement of this role. There is no Japanese language ability (a hard requirement at native level), no evidence of Linux kernel analysis or modification, and no background in network security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or switch firmware. His C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, with his career since centered on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms rather than embedded networking. Strong general engineering skill does not offset the missing domain-specific and language prerequisites.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Some low-level exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang systems, hardware work with robotic arms
Solid Linux user-space experience and comfort across diverse stacks
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a mandatory native-level requirement
No Linux kernel modification, embedded firmware, or L2/L3 switch development experience
No exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal, tamper detection)

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
